dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3690 IS: Ensuring Access to Medicaid Buy-in Programs Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Blackburn introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend title XIX of the Social Security Act to remove certain age restrictions on Medicaid eligibility for working adults with disabilities.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Ensuring Access to Medicaid Buy-in Programs Act of 2026.

section.header: Removing certain age restrictions on Medicaid eligibility for working adults with disabilities

paragraph.text: Section 1902(a)(10)(A)(ii) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396a(a)(10)(A)(ii)) is amended—

clause.text: by striking , but less than 65,; and
by inserting , including at least the group described in section 1905(a)(xviii) before the semicolon at the end; and

subparagraph.text: in subclause (XVI), by inserting including at least the group described in section 1905(a)(xii), after the State may establish,.

paragraph.text: Section 1905(a) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396d(a)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in clause (xvi), by striking or at the end;
in clause (xvii), by adding or after the comma at the end; and
by adding after clause (xvii) the following new clause:

clause.text: individuals who, but for earnings in excess of the limit established under subsection (q)(2)(B), would be considered to be receiving supplemental security income, and who are at least 16 years of age,

paragraph.text: Section 1905(v)(1)(A) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396d(v)(1)(A)) is amended by striking , but less than 65,.

subsection.text: A State that, as of the date of enactment of this Act, provides for making medical assistance available to individuals described in subclause (XV) or (XVI) of section 1902(a)(10)(A)(ii) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396a(a)(10)(A)(ii)) shall not be regarded as failing to comply with the requirements of the amendments made by subsection (a) before January 1, 2028.
